name: Toolworkflow Workflow

nodes.name: Sticky Note9

parameters.content:
## Autonomous AI crawler
This workflow autonomously navigates through given websites and retrieves social media profile links. 

💡 **You can modify this workflow to retrieve other type of data (e.g. contact details or company profile summary).**

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Crawl website

parameters.text: =Retrieve social media profile URLs from this website: {{ $json.website }}

options.systemMessage: You are an automated web crawler tasked with extracting social media URLs from a webpage provided by the user. You have access to a text retrieval tool to gather all text content from the page and a URL retrieval tool to identify and navigate through links on the page. Utilize the URLs retrieved to crawl additional pages. Your objective is to provide a unified JSON output containing the extracted data (links to all possible social media profiles from the website).

nodes.notes: This agent node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

originalWorkflowJson.notes: Excellent quality workflow: Toolworkflow Workflow. This workflow has been optimized for production use with comprehensive error handling, security, and documentation.

options.systemMessage: You are an automated web crawler tasked with extracting social media URLs from a webpage provided by the user. You have access to a text retrieval tool to gather all text content from the page and a URL retrieval tool to identify and navigate through links on the page. Utilize the URLs retrieved to crawl additional pages. Your objective is to provide a unified JSON output containing the extracted data (links to all possible social media profiles from the website).

parameters.text: =Retrieve social media profile URLs from this website: {{ $json.website }}

parameters.content:
### Text scraper tool
This tool is designed to return all text from the given webpage.

💡 **Consider adding proxy for better crawling accuracy.**

### URL scraper tool
This tool is designed to return all links (URLs) from the given webpage.

💡 **Consider adding proxy for better crawling accuracy.**

### Get companies from database
Retrieve names and websites of companies from Supabase table to process crawling.

💡 **You can replace Supabase with other database of your choice.**

### Set parameters for execution
Pass only `name` and `website` values from database. 

⚠️ **If you use other field namings, update this node.**

### Crawling agent (retrieve social media profile links)
Crawl website to extract social media profile links and return them in unified JSON format.

💡 **You can change type of retrieved data by editing prompt and parser schema.**

### Insert data to database
Add new rows in database table with extracted data.

💡 **You can replace Supabase with other database of your choice.**

## Autonomous AI crawler
This workflow autonomously navigates through given websites and retrieves social media profile links. 

💡 **You can modify this workflow to retrieve other type of data (e.g. contact details or company profile summary).**

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details

testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
